daily.bias_reason: Nifty 50 last closed Thursday June 25 at 24,056 (+0.14%, +34 pts) as India was in a 3-day weekend (Muharram on Friday June 26 + Saturday June 27); Brent crude extended its collapse to $72.60/bbl by Friday (down 4.8% on the day, -20% for June — the biggest monthly drop since March 2020), while global risk-off returned with MSCI Asia Pacific falling 1.7% and South Korea's Kospi dropping 3%+ on semiconductor weakness. The dominant signal is the tension between India's powerful domestic macro tailwind (cheap crude, VIX 13.05, FII turning net buyers for the first time in weeks at +₹384 Cr on Jun 25) and a deteriorating global risk backdrop — US equity futures pointed lower ahead of the long weekend, raising the probability of a gap-down Monday open despite Nifty sitting comfortably above the 50-DMA (~23,843).
daily.watch: Watch Monday open for: (1) whether FII delivers a 2nd consecutive net-buy print — the T1 Capitulation Reversal setup requires 2-3 consecutive days to activate; (2) Nifty's ability to defend 24,000 support against global tech/semiconductor headwinds (MSCI Asia -1.7%, Korea -3%); (3) Brent crude stability near $72/bbl — a close below $70 would be the next major support and could test the Iran ceasefire narrative; (4) any India-specific macro data or RBI commentary.

nifty.note: Jun 25 close (last trading day — Jun 26 Muharram holiday, Jun 27 Saturday). +34.35 pts. Third consecutive weekly gain. OHLC Jun 25: O=24,125.85 H=24,261.60 C=24,056. Above 50-DMA ~23,843. Sensex at 77,100.47. Source: Livemint/Investing.com.

vix.note: India VIX Jun 25 close: 13.05 (down from 13.39 prev). Confirmed by Investing.com historical data. Third consecutive session decline. Well below 15 — low fear, calm options market. VIX 10-day trend: 12.84 (Jun 22), 12.97 (Jun 19).

usdinr.note: USD/INR spot: 94.3605 (Yahoo Finance, Jun 27 Saturday rate). Slight INR strength vs Jun 25 close of 94.40. Effectively flat. Rupee holding despite global risk-off weekend. RBI supportive measures noted in Livemint.

pcr.note: NSE Option Chain API continues to block direct PCR fetch — 9th+ consecutive session unavailable. VIX at 13.05 and post-expiry context (Jun 25 was weekly F&O expiry) suggests options market was calm. Next PCR available Monday.

breadth.note: BSE breadth Jun 25: 1,656 advanced / 2,556 declined / 194 unchanged. Advance ratio 37.6% — firmly bearish zone (<45%). Nifty headline +0.14% masked broader weakness. Decliners led by metals (-1.37%), IT (-0.86%), ONGC, Hindalco, Power Grid.

fii.note: FII net bought Rs383.76 Cr on Jun 25 — FIRST confirmed net-buy print after multi-week selling streak. DII bought Rs5,747.75 Cr. T1 Capitulation Reversal watch now active: Day 1 of potential reversal. Need 2nd consecutive buy on Monday for first confirmation.

crude.note: Brent crude: Jun 25 close $75.50, dropped to $72.60 on Jun 26 (Friday, -$3.6 or -4.8%) as Strait of Hormuz transit improved post US-Iran ceasefire. June monthly decline ~20% — biggest since March 2020. Strong India macro tailwind (auto, aviation, OMCs). Source: Investing.com historical + Livemint.

mmi.note: Market Mood Index: 65.47 (Greed zone), updated Jun 25 14:20 IST. Confirmed by Tickertape live data ('currentValue: 65.47, date: 2026-06-25T14:20:00'). Showing 'Updated 1 day ago' as of Jun 27 morning. Greed maintained despite negative breadth — driven by crude tailwind and 3-week winning streak.

weekly.summary: Nifty closed the holiday-shortened week at 24,056 (+0.18% vs prior Friday), defending 24,000 across four sessions despite a bearish BSE breadth print (only 37.6% advancers Thursday). The honest read: this was a narrow, large-cap rally carried by private banks while FMCG, metals and IT lagged — index strength masking broad-market fatigue. The real engine remains intermarket and flows, not breadth. Brent collapsed to ~$72.6 (a ~20% June drop, pre-war levels as Hormuz transit normalised and JP Morgan cut its H2 forecast), handing India its best disinflation tailwind in months, while DII bought a colossal ~₹11,100 Cr to absorb continued FII cash selling of ~₹2,077 Cr. The non-obvious tension: FII cash stayed negative all week, yet on Friday FIIs aggressively built index-futures longs (long-short ratio spiked to ~13.9) — derivatives positioning is turning bullish before cash confirms, a classic pre-reversal divergence that can resolve violently either way. With FII ownership at a 14-year low of 14.7%, the marginal seller is nearly exhausted. Posture for next week: constructive but unconfirmed. Cheap crude plus financials leadership justifies holding longs above 24,000; but until FII cash flips net-positive for 2-3 sessions and breadth broadens, conviction stays capped — buy dips toward 23,800-23,900, respect 24,260 resistance.
weekly.market_posture: Constructive on macro and DII bid, unconfirmed on FII cash — hold 24k, buy dips to 23,800, but await FII cash turn and breadth broadening before adding aggressively.

threads.name: FII Exodus — Structural Outflow
threads.pov: FIIs stayed net sellers in cash this week (~-₹2,077 Cr) with ownership at a 14-year low of 14.7%, so the outflow is not over — but Friday's sharp index-futures long build-up (L/S ratio ~13.9) signals positioning is quietly turning before cash. The structural bear thesis is intact but losing momentum as cheap crude and a firm rupee erode the exodus rationale.
threads.watch_next_week: FII provisional cash print Monday/Tuesday — 2-3 consecutive net-buy days activates T1 Capitulation Reversal; whether the futures L/S build-up converts into cash buying or unwinds. A single >₹5,000 Cr cash sell day flips this back to red.

threads.name: RBI Rate Cycle — Transmission Lag
threads.pov: The ~20% June crude crash and a steady rupee near 94.3 materially ease the inflation and INR constraints on RBI, and private banks led the index this week — consistent with a market pricing easier policy transmission. Thesis intact and incrementally more favourable.
threads.watch_next_week: Banking/financials follow-through and credit-growth commentary; any RBI signal on liquidity or the INR-vs-growth trade-off; whether cheaper crude shows up in softer inflation expectations.

threads.name: Monsoon 2026 Onset
threads.pov: No fresh IMD weekly rainfall data was retrievable, leaving onset progress unconfirmed, and FMCG conspicuously lagged this week — a market proxy hinting rural-consumption conviction is still soft. Thesis unchanged but the lagging consumer tape is a mild yellow flag.
threads.watch_next_week: IMD weekly rainfall vs long-period average and core-zone coverage; FMCG/two-wheeler/agri-input price action as a recovery proxy; any pickup in rural-facing names alongside the broader rally.

weekly_povs.summary: Dashboard initialized. First weekly synthesis will run Saturday June 14, 2026.
weekly_povs.market_posture: Cautious. FII selling persistent. Hold reduced leverage until FII flow turns or RBI/monsoon provides positive catalyst.

threads.name: FII Exodus — Structural Outflow
threads.pov: FII sold ₹76,408 Cr in 30 days — likely driven by DXY strength and EM rotation to China. DII SIP flows are the floor. T1 Capitulation Reversal setup forming. When FII turns, reversal will be sharp.
threads.watch_next_week: Daily FII cash — 3 consecutive buy days = T1 model activates. Monitor FII L/S ratio on NSE Bhavcopy.

threads.name: RBI Rate Cycle — Transmission Lag
threads.pov: RBI cuts are in system (repo at 5.75%) but credit growth and housing recovery are lagging. June MPC outcome + INR trajectory will determine if easing continues or pauses.
threads.watch_next_week: June MPC decision and RBI governor language on INR vs. growth trade-off. Banking/NBFC stocks reaction.
sources: RBI policy history, backtest W01 Dec 2025

threads.name: Monsoon 2026 Onset
threads.pov: June onset month. Normal/above-normal monsoon validates rural consumption recovery (FMCG, two-wheelers, agri). Deficit extends rural squeeze and caps Nifty upside in consumer names.
threads.watch_next_week: IMD forecast update. Kerala/Karnataka onset timing. Rainfall vs. long-period average.
sources: IMD seasonal outlook, seasonal pattern analysis

weekly_povs.summary: Nifty reclaimed 24,000 this week, closing at 24,013 (+1.65%) after tagging an intraweek high of 24,189 on Thursday, then fading into Friday alongside Bank Nifty (57,686, also red on Friday). The real driver was intermarket, not flows: WTI crude collapsed ~10% to $76.5 (Brent $80.6) and USDINR firmed to ~94.3 even as the dollar index pushed to a three-week high near 100.85. That INR-up-while-DXY-up divergence is the week's most important tell — it implies crude relief and genuine inflow are absorbing dollar strength, quietly undercutting the FII-exodus narrative. The non-obvious risk: this entire reclaim happened with zero confirmed FII buy data, so the marginal buyer is unverified — likely DII SIPs plus short-covering. If DXY keeps climbing past 101, the crude tailwind gets overwhelmed and FII selling can resume into resistance. Friday's twin fade after a five-day run argues the easy money is made. Posture: constructive but unconfirmed — the macro backdrop (cheap crude, firm INR) is the best in weeks, but conviction stays capped until FII flow prints positive.
weekly_povs.market_posture: Constructive on macro, unconfirmed on flows — hold 24k, but await FII buy-print before adding longs into resistance.

threads.name: FII Exodus — Structural Outflow
threads.pov: Intermarket evidence shifted against the bear thesis this week: INR strengthened to ~94.3 despite a rising dollar, and the index reclaimed 24k — behavior inconsistent with active heavy selling. But no confirmed FII provisional buy data printed all week, so the outflow cannot be declared over; upgrading red to yellow on improving conditions, not resolution.
threads.watch_next_week: First confirmed FII provisional cash print Monday. 3 consecutive net-buy days activates the T1 Capitulation Reversal setup; a single >₹5,000 Cr sell day flips it back to red.

threads.name: RBI Rate Cycle — Transmission Lag
threads.pov: The ~10% crude crash and a firming rupee materially ease the inflation/INR constraint on RBI, giving the easing path more room without defending the currency. Thesis intact and incrementally more favorable; banks led most of the week (Bank Nifty +1.5%) before Friday's fade.
threads.watch_next_week: Any RBI commentary on INR vs. growth; banking/NBFC follow-through after Friday's pullback; whether cheaper crude shows up in inflation expectations.

threads.name: Monsoon 2026 Onset
threads.pov: No fresh IMD weekly data was retrievable this week, leaving onset progress unconfirmed. Thesis unchanged: normal/above-normal rainfall validates the rural-consumption recovery; any deficit caps consumer-name upside. Holding yellow pending data.
threads.watch_next_week: IMD weekly rainfall vs. long-period average and core-monsoon-zone coverage; FMCG/two-wheeler/agri-input price action as a market proxy.
